commit.message:
fix: call test-associate after key exchange and handle broadcast messages (#2)

Three protocol-level fixes:

1. _ensure_session() now calls test_associate() after change_public_keys().
   KeePassXC resets its internal m_associated flag on every key exchange, so
   authenticated requests (get-logins, set-login, lock-database, etc.) all
   return error 8 (Association failed) unless test-associate is sent first.
   A new _associated bool tracks this per-session state.

2. _send_json() now uses JSONDecoder.raw_decode() instead of json.loads() to
   parse only the first JSON object from the recv buffer. KeePassXC sends an
   unsolicited 'database-locked' broadcast immediately after the lock-database
   response, producing concatenated JSON that json.loads() rejects with
   'Extra data'.

3. Error responses in _send_encrypted() are now logged at WARNING level
   (was DEBUG) so callers see the actual KeePassXC error message.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename WI_ platform API to ax equivalents

Replace platform-specific WI_* symbols with the new ax* / AX_* names across the codebase. Changes include renaming event types (WI_Message -> AXmessage), key/modifier constants (WI_KEY_ -> AX_KEY_, WI_MOD_ -> AX_MOD_), timing and window APIs (WI_GetMilliseconds, WI_Init, WI_Shutdown, WI_CreateWindow, WI_BeginPaint, WI_EndPaint, WI_PollEvent, WI_WaitEvent, WI_PostMessageW, etc.) to their ax counterparts, plus related scaling/size/joystick functions. Updated documentation, examples, tests, kernel, commctl, and user modules to match the new platform API naming.

* Fix WinAPI name conflicts and Windows test crash

- commctl/msgbox.h: wrap MB_OK/OKCANCEL/YESNO/YESNOCANCEL and IDOK/CANCEL/YES/NO in #ifndef guards to suppress redefinition warnings when compiled alongside <winuser.h>
- user/accel.h: wrap FVIRTKEY/FSHIFT/FCONTROL/FALT in #ifndef guards (same reason)
- kernel/init.c: add g_graphics_initialized flag; guard ui_begin_frame()/ui_end_frame() so they're no-ops when platform not initialized, preventing crash in headless unit tests (button_click_test, toolbar_*_test)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/corepunch/orion-ui/sessions/c190dd6d-33d6-426a-94a2-01d9b75673b6

Co-authored-by: corepunch <83646194+corepunch@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Windows test crash: skip paint messages in repost_messages() when !running

create_window() calls invalidate_window() which posts kWindowMessagePaint
and kWindowMessageNonClientPaint. When repost_messages() dispatches these
to window procs (e.g. win_button), they call fill_rect()/draw_text_small()
which use OpenGL function pointers. On Windows without glewInit() these
pointers are NULL, causing a crash with no output.

Fix: skip both paint message types in repost_messages() when !running
(consistent with existing kWindowMessageRefreshStencil guard).

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/corepunch/orion-ui/sessions/e20d4acb-77f3-44bf-8b71-3e7ca3b9b7b1

Co-authored-by: corepunch <83646194+corepunch@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reset UI state and deduplicate posted messages

Add test_env_reset_ui_state to clear global UI state (destroy windows, cleanup hooks, reset message queue, clear focus/track/capture, stop running) and call it from test init/shutdown to isolate tests. Introduce reset_message_queue() to zero the global message queue and expose it in user.h. Change post_message() to avoid enqueueing duplicate (target,msg) pairs by returning early instead of nullifying existing entries. Also include <stdarg.h> in user/window.c for required vararg support.

* Fix event_loop_test failures and Windows DLL loading for tests

commit.message:
fix: preserve key order in YAML output (#2034)

* fix: preserve key order in YAML output

MlrmapToYAMLNative converted the ordered Mlrmap to map[string]interface{},
which loses insertion order. yaml.v3.Marshal then sorts Go map keys
alphabetically, so YAML output always had sorted keys despite other formats
(JSON, pretty-print) correctly preserving order.

Switch to building yaml.Node trees that preserve the Mlrmap's linked-list
iteration order. Use yaml.Node.Encode for scalar values to correctly handle
edge cases (NaN/Inf, non-UTF-8 strings).

Fixes #2028.

* Update pkg/mlrval/mlrval_yaml_test.go with copilot code-review feedback

Add error boundary and auto-remediation for TV video player (#28)

The TV display can end up on an unrecoverable black screen when the
YouTube embed fails to load or stalls silently, requiring a manual page
refresh with no on-screen indication of what went wrong.

### Changes

- **React error boundary** (`tv-error-boundary.tsx`): Catches rendering
crashes in the TV player subtree. Shows a full-screen error UI with a
"Reload page" button that auto-clicks after a 5-second countdown. Any
user interaction (mouse/keyboard/touch) pauses the countdown to hand
control back to a human.

- **Video stall detection** (`tv-player.tsx`): Monitors `timeupdate`
events while `playing` is true. If no progress is reported for 15
seconds, assumes the embed is stuck and reloads the page.

- **Video error handler** (`tv-player.tsx`): Listens for `onError` from
the video element and triggers a reload after 3 seconds.

- **Wiring** (`tv-display.tsx`): Wraps `<TVPlayer>` in
`<TVErrorBoundary>`.

> **Note:** rwsdk's RSC bundler only recognizes function exports from
`"use client"` modules, so the class-based error boundary is wrapped in
an exported function component.

fix(ast_lowering): use full param span for enum/struct destructuring patterns

When a function parameter is an enum/struct destructuring pattern such as
`Page { title, alerts, posts }`, `get_param_names` correctly lowercased the
struct name to use as the synthesised parameter name (e.g. `page`), but then
only looked for `PatKind::Identifier` spans when computing `source_span`.
Enum patterns fell through to `unwrap_or_default()`, yielding `Span::default()`
(position 0,0), so inlay hints for these parameters were placed at the very
beginning of the file.

Fix by also handling `PatKind::Enum` in the source-span lookup, using the
full `FunctionParameter.span` (which covers the entire pattern including the
closing `}`).  This places the type hint right after the closing brace, which
is the natural position for an annotation following a destructured parameter.

Add a regression test in `tlang_analysis` that verifies no hint from a struct
pattern parameter lands at position (0, 0).

Phase 14: Shimmer Animation (#33)

## Summary

**Phase 14: Shimmer Animation**
**Goal:** Users see a smooth animated skeleton shimmer while translation
is in progress, with no jarring layout shifts
**Status:** Verified ✓ (3/3 must-haves passed)

Adds a polished left-to-right gradient sweep shimmer animation to the
popup loading state. The shimmer plays from translation start until the
result appears, using `.plusLighter` blend mode that adapts to both
light and dark system appearance. Implemented as a zero-layout-impact
`.overlay` ViewModifier to avoid interfering with the popup's
GeometryReader height measurement. Fully respects the Reduce Motion
accessibility setting.

## Changes

### Plan 14-01: ShimmerModifier ViewModifier + Unit Tests
Left-to-right gradient sweep ViewModifier using `.plusLighter` blend
mode with Reduce Motion accessibility fallback.

**Key files created:**
- `Transy/Popup/ShimmerModifier.swift` — ViewModifier with animated
gradient sweep, `.clipped()`, `.allowsHitTesting(false)`
- `TransyTests/ShimmerModifierTests.swift` — 3 structural tests

### Plan 14-02: Wire Shimmer into LoadingPopupText
Connected `ShimmerModifier` to the actual loading UI via `.shimmer()` on
`PopupText` inside `LoadingPopupText.body`.

**Key files modified:**
- `Transy/Popup/PopupView.swift` — Added `.shimmer()` call on
loading-state text

## Requirements Addressed

| ID | Requirement | Status |
|----|------------|--------|
| SHM-01 | Shimmer animation plays on popup text during translation
loading | ✓ |
| SHM-02 | Zero layout impact — no resize notification storm | ✓ |
| SHM-03 | Reduce Motion disables animation — static muted text fallback
| ✓ |

## Verification

- [x] Automated verification: PASSED (3/3 must-haves)
- [x] Human visual verification: gradient sweep, no layout shift, Reduce
Motion compliance
- [x] Unit tests: all pass (`make test`)

## Key Decisions

- `.overlay` architecture (not ZStack) to prevent GeometryReader height
measurement interference
- `.plusLighter` blend mode for light/dark adaptive shimmer appearance
- 1.5s linear `repeatForever(autoreverses: false)` sweep speed
- `.onAppear` for animation start (not `.task`) per pitfall analysis

Closes #28

commit.message:
fix(macos): fix clipboard copy failing from tray and GUI (#4629)

The clipboard copy from tray menu and capture GUI was broken on macOS:

1. FlameshotDaemon::copyToClipboard() created a KDSingleApplication
   instance and called isPrimaryInstance(), which always returned false
   (the daemon already holds the socket). This caused a 3-second IPC
   timeout before falling back. Fix: use instance() to check if the
   daemon singleton exists in-process, matching non-macOS behavior.

2. saveToClipboardMime() used setData("image/...") which puts a lazy
   reference on the macOS pasteboard. If the app exits before paste,
   data is lost ("Cannot keep promise"). Fix: use setImageData() for
   native pasteboard persistence, plus setData() for exact format.

3. saveJpegToClipboardMacOS() used osascript with a temp file and
   incorrectly labeled JPEG data as PNG UTI. Removed entirely — the
   fixed saveToClipboardMime() now handles both JPEG and PNG.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Feat/add keepassxc cli (#1)

* Add keepassxc-cli formula template, update script, and workflow

- scripts/keepassxc-cli/formula.rb.j2: Homebrew formula template
- scripts/keepassxc-cli/update_formula.py: PyPI dependency resolver
- .github/workflows/update-keepassxc-cli.yml: dispatch-triggered update
- Formula/keepassxc-cli.rb will be generated on first PyPI publish

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: single shared update_formula.py script

Replace per-package update_formula.py scripts with a single shared
script at scripts/update_formula.py that takes <package-name> <version>.
Templates stay per-package in scripts/<package-name>/formula.rb.j2.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: move Jinja templates to formula-templates/

Separate concerns: scripts/ for executable scripts,
formula-templates/<package>/ for Jinja templates.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resource names (hyphens), trim Jinja blocks, regenerate formulas

- update_formula.py: keep hyphens in dep names (brew audit requires
  resource names to match PyPI package names with hyphens)
- update_formula.py: add trim_blocks=True to Jinja environment to
  prevent extra blank lines (brew style Layout/EmptyLines violation)
- Regenerate keepassxc-ssh-agent formula for v1.1.0 with new
  keepassxc-browser-api==0.1.0 dependency
- Regenerate keepassxc-cli formula for v0.1.0 (initial)
- Add .gitignore

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: make keepassxc-cli macOS-only in formula

Matches keepassxc-ssh-agent behavior — biometric unlock (TouchID)
is a macOS feature. Avoids libffi linkage failures on Linux.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct dependency order in keepassxc-cli formula

brew audit requires dependencies in alphabetical order:
libsodium → macos → python@3.13

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: purge soft-deleted Key Vault before terraform to prevent auto-recovery conflict

Root cause: when a KV is soft-deleted (not purged) and Terraform tries to
create one with the same derived name, Azure auto-recovers the deleted vault
including all its secrets. Terraform state is empty so it fails with
'resource already exists' for each secret, which also blocks the variable
group creation that depends on them.

Changes:
- azure-pipelines.yml: add 'Purge Soft-Deleted Key Vault' step (AzureCLI@2)
  before Terraform Init. Derives the vault name using the same formula as
  local.kv_name, checks az keyvault list-deleted, and if found runs
  'az keyvault purge' synchronously with a poll loop (up to 200s) to confirm
  completion before Terraform runs. No-op if no soft-deleted vault exists.

The import step added previously is kept as a safety net for cases where
the vault was not soft-deleted but secrets existed from a partial run.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
